bids.reasoning: This is the opening bid at t=0, so I should start with my maximum utility offer. My most important issue is issueD (0.4 weight), followed by issueC (0.3), issueB (0.2), and issueA (0.1). For all issues, valueF gives the highest utility of 1.0. I'll open with all valueF to maximize my utility at the start of the negotiation.

It's very early (t=0.02) and the opponent countered with all-A values (worst for me, utility ~0.20 across all issues), which is their opening anchor. Their message is aggressive but that's cheap talk. I should concede slightly from my perfect opening while staying high, focusing on maintaining my most important issues (D and C). I'll drop issueA (lowest weight) to something mid-range as a gesture, but keep D and C high.

The opponent is repeatedly bidding all-valueA (their best, my worst) and using aggressive language. It's very early (t=0.04), so I don't need to concede much. My highest-weight issues are issueD (0.4) and issueC (0.3), so I should hold firm there. I can concede a bit more on issueA (lowest weight) and issueB. I'll move slightly more toward the opponent on issueB while keeping issueC and issueD at high values, targeting a utility around 0.85+.

The opponent has been bidding all-valueA on everything (their best, my worst), except their latest offer moved issueD to valueB - a tiny concession. Their messages are aggressive but their bids show they're barely moving. It's very early (t=0.06), so I should hold firm on my high-weight issues (issueD and issueC) while making modest concessions on lower-weight issues (issueA and issueB). My current bid utility: issueA=valueD(0.068), issueB=valueD(0.136), issueC=valueF(0.3), issueD=valueF(0.4) = ~0.904. I'll keep issueC and issueD at valueF (most important) and make a small concession on issueB to valueC to show movement without sacrificing much.

The opponent is stuck at valueA for almost everything, only moving issueD to valueB in their last two offers. Their aggressive messages are cheap talk contradicted by their near-static bidding. We're only at t=0.08, very early, so I should continue to hold firm on my high-weight issues (issueD and issueC) while making modest concessions on lower-weight issues (issueA, issueB). My previous bid was issueA: valueD, issueB: valueC, issueC: valueF, issueD: valueF. I'll slightly concede on issueB and issueA while keeping issueC and issueD at high values, to avoid repeating the same bid.

My most important issues are issueD (0.4) and issueC (0.3), so I should hold firm on those. The opponent seems to care about issueC and issueD based on their complaints. I'll slightly concede on issueA and issueB (low importance to me) while maintaining strong values on issueC and issueD. I'll keep issueC at valueF and issueD at valueF, but vary issueA and issueB a bit to avoid repeating the exact same bid.
